reflection.theme: The life you build on ordinary Wednesdays
reflection.quote: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
reflection.reading:
It's a Wednesday in early June. Nothing about today is going to feel cinematic. You're not getting a chip, you're not standing on a mountain, you're not closing a chapter. You're just going to wake up, do the work in front of you, eat something, talk to a few people, and go to bed sober. That's the whole assignment.

When I was drinking, I thought a good life was made of peaks — the trip, the party, the night that became a story. Sobriety taught me something almost embarrassingly simple: a good life is mostly Wednesdays. It's the run you didn't want to do. The text you sent back instead of leaving on read. The dishes. The boring half hour where nothing happens and you don't need anything to happen. Those are the bricks. The trip to Whistler, the friendships that actually hold, the version of yourself you can stand to be alone with — all of it gets built on days that don't announce themselves.

So today, lower the bar on meaning and raise the bar on showing up. You don't have to feel inspired. You don't have to feel anything in particular. You just have to be the person who did the next right thing on an ordinary June Wednesday. That person, repeated, becomes someone you'll be glad to know.
reflection.meditation: What is one small, unglamorous thing I can do today that the future version of me would thank me for?

affirmations: I am building a life out of ordinary days, and that is enough.
Today I get to choose my response instead of being run by my reaction.
I am becoming someone I can actually live with.

gratitude.prayer: I'm grateful for a quiet Wednesday and a clear head to meet it with. I'm grateful for the people who picked up the phone when I was new, and for the ones in my life now who only know me sober. Most of all, I'm grateful that today I get to be present for my own life — the coffee, the work, the small conversations — instead of hiding from it.
gratitude.closing: One more day, one more chance to show up. That's the whole gift.
